properties.name: githound-workflow-test\Test 5 - OIDC Token Access
properties.contents:
name: "Test 5 - OIDC Token Access"
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - test/oidc-token

permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read

jobs:
  check-oidc:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Request OIDC token and inspect claims
        run: |
          echo "=== Test 5: OIDC Token Access ==="

          if [ -n "$ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL" ]; then
            echo "OIDC endpoint IS available"

            TOKEN=$(curl -s \
              -H "Authorization: bearer $ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_TOKEN" \
              "${ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL}&audience=sts.amazonaws.com" \
              | jq -r '.value')

            if [ "$TOKEN" != "null" ] && [ -n "$TOKEN" ]; then
              echo "RESULT: OIDC token successfully obtained"
              PAYLOAD=$(echo "$TOKEN" | cut -d'.' -f2 | tr '_-' '/+' | awk '{while(length%4)$0=$0"="}1' | base64 -d)
              echo "JWT claims:"
              echo "$PAYLOAD" | jq . || echo "$PAYLOAD"
            else
              echo "RESULT: OIDC token request failed"
            fi
          else
            echo "RESULT: OIDC endpoint NOT available"
          fi

properties.name: githound-workflow-test\Test 4 - GITHUB_TOKEN Write Permissions
properties.contents:
name: "Test 4 - GITHUB_TOKEN Write Permissions"
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - test/token-write-perms

jobs:
  check-token-default:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Check default token permissions
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          echo "=== Test 4a: Default GITHUB_TOKEN Permissions ==="

          TAG_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -X POST \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $GH_TOKEN" \
            -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
            "https://api.github.com/repos/${{ github.repository }}/git/refs" \
            -d '{"ref":"refs/tags/test-default-perms","sha":"${{ github.sha }}"}')
          echo "Tag creation (contents:write): HTTP $TAG_RESPONSE"

          ISSUE_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -X POST \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $GH_TOKEN" \
            -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
            "https://api.github.com/repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues" \
            -d '{"title":"Test 4a - default token write test"}')
          echo "Issue creation (issues:write): HTTP $ISSUE_RESPONSE"

          echo "201 = has write, 403 = read-only"

  check-token-explicit:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      issues: write
    steps:
      - name: Check explicit write permissions
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          echo "=== Test 4b: Explicit Write Permissions ==="

          TAG_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -X POST \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $GH_TOKEN" \
            -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
            "https://api.github.com/repos/${{ github.repository }}/git/refs" \
            -d '{"ref":"refs/tags/test-explicit-perms","sha":"${{ github.sha }}"}')
          echo "Tag creation (contents:write): HTTP $TAG_RESPONSE"

          ISSUE_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -X POST \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $GH_TOKEN" \
            -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
            "https://api.github.com/repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues" \
            -d '{"title":"Test 4b - explicit token write test"}')
          echo "Issue creation (issues:write): HTTP $ISSUE_RESPONSE"

          echo "201 = has write, 403 = read-only"

properties.name: githound-workflow-test\Test 3 - Environment Secret Access
properties.contents:
name: "Test 3 - Environment Secret Access"
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - test/env-secret-access

jobs:
  check-env-secret:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - name: Check if environment secret is accessible
        env:
          SECRET: ${{ secrets.ENV_SECRET_TEST }}
        run: |
          echo "=== Test 3: Environment Secret Access ==="
          if [ -n "$SECRET" ]; then
            HASH=$(echo -n "$SECRET" | sha256sum | cut -d' ' -f1)
            echo "RESULT: Environment secret IS accessible"
            echo "SHA256: $HASH"
          else
            echo "RESULT: Environment secret is NOT accessible (empty)"
          fi

properties.name: oidc-actions-test-1\AWS Role Assumption Test - WildCard
properties.contents:
name: AWS Role Assumption Test - WildCard

on:
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read

jobs:
  assume-role:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Get and Display OIDC Token
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const token = await core.getIDToken('sigstore');
            
            // Token will be masked automatically
            console.log('Full Token:', token);
            
            // Decode and display claims
            const [header, payload, signature] = token.split('.');
            const decodedPayload = JSON.parse(
              Buffer.from(payload, 'base64').toString('utf-8')
            );
            
            console.log('Token Claims:', JSON.stringify(decodedPayload, null, 2));

      - name: Configure AWS credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::851725637490:role/GithubActionsTest
          aws-region: us-west-2
          role-session-name: GitHubActions

      - name: Verify AWS identity
        run: |
          echo "Getting caller identity..."
          aws sts get-caller-identity
          
          echo ""
          echo "Successfully assumed role!"
          echo "UserId: $(aws sts get-caller-identity --query UserId --output text)"
          echo "Account: $(aws sts get-caller-identity --query Account --output text)"
          echo "Arn: $(aws sts get-caller-identity --query Arn --output text)"

properties.name: CircleCI App
properties.description:
### CircleCI’s GitHub App Integration
For information, navigate to [CircleCI’s docs](https://circleci.com/docs/github-apps-integration/)

properties.name: GitHound-Collector
properties.description:
# GitHound Collector - GitHub App

**Security Graph Visualization for Your GitHub Organization**

GitHound Collector is a read-only GitHub app that automatically gathers organizational structure and permission data from your GitHub organization for analysis in BloodHound. It transforms your GitHub environment into an interactive security graph, enabling you to visualize access paths, audit permissions, and identify potential security risks.

## What It Collects

The app collects comprehensive data about your GitHub organization's security posture:

- **Organizations**: Your GitHub org metadata and structure
- **Users**: Individual accounts and their organizational relationships
- **Teams**: Team hierarchies and group memberships
- **Repositories**: All repos within your organization
- **Branches**: Named branches and their protection rules
- **Roles & Permissions**: Organization-level, team-level, and repository-level roles and access rights

## Use Cases

- **Access Audits**: Instantly visualize who has admin, write, or read access to repositories and branches
- **Compliance Validation**: Verify least-privilege principles across your organization
- **Security Reviews**: Identify privilege escalation paths and excessive permissions
- **Incident Response**: Rapidly trace compromised accounts and their potential impact

## Required Permissions

GitHound requires **read-only** access to:

### Repository Permissions
- Administrator (read)
- Contents (read)
- Metadata (read)

### Organization Permissions
- Administrator (read)
- Custom organization roles (read)
- Custom repository roles (read)
- Members (read)

## How It Works

1. Install the app to your GitHub organization
2. Grant read-only permissions to all repositories
3. The app collects organizational structure and permission data
4. Export the data as JSON for import into BloodHound
5. Visualize and analyze your GitHub security graph

## Privacy & Security

- **Read-only**: GitHound never modifies your repositories or settings
- **No code access**: Only metadata and permission structures are collected
- **Local processing**: All analysis happens in your BloodHound instance
- **Open source**: Fully transparent and auditable code
